title: Implement backend catalog query and filter contract
verify: Backend catalog query supports category, q, price_min, price_max, color, material, size_length, product_type, mounting_method, page, and limit.
Search and filters can be combined and return empty results without error.
Response includes enough data for product cards, category navigation, filter state, available values, empty state, and pagination.
evidence_required: Integration test output for category, search, filters, combined filters, and empty results.
Short note identifying native Medusa route versus thin read-only facade decision.
constraints: Use Medusa/PostgreSQL-backed search/filtering or supported Medusa mechanisms.
Use explicit query parameters for filters and pagination.
Do not expose internal database identifiers when stable public identifiers/handles are available.
invariants: Do not introduce an external search engine for MVP.
Backend/PostgreSQL owns catalog source of truth.
purpose: Expose the backend catalog read behavior needed by the storefront without duplicating catalog truth.
success_outcome: Backend catalog reads prove required browse, search, and filter semantics against seeded data.
anti_goals: Do not implement FT-002 product detail behavior.
Do not add external search infrastructure.
Do not add cart, checkout, payment, or auth behavior.

stop_conditions: Native Medusa APIs cannot satisfy required filter semantics and no thin facade can be scoped safely.
Endpoint contract would conflict with API guidelines.
Verification cannot prove filters/search against seeded backend data.
